runtimeJobsMayMutateLater: admin Stripe product setup for live prices
admin Stripe product setup for test prices
Render environment variable updates for Stripe price IDs
Render deploy hook trigger after env updates
license creation, extension, and subscription period sync
gift code creation, direct package creation, and redemption
admin product, price, coupon, and promotion-code management
backupExportRequiredBeforeRuntimeJobs: Export or snapshot the production database before any admin runtime setup, webhook replay, checkout verification, gift/license migration, or subscription sync.
Capture current Render environment variable names and whether required values are set, without exporting secret values into artifacts.
Capture current Stripe product and price IDs for the three public paid plans before creating or updating runtime envs.
Capture current webhook endpoint configuration and event delivery status before any replay or deploy verification.
rollbackNotesForProductSetup: The setup script must only find or create 3days, monthly, and lifetime products/prices; it must not delete or deactivate old Stripe products.
If a wrong price ID is written to Render, restore the previous Render env value from the pre-runtime env inventory and redeploy.
If a duplicate product or price is created, leave it inactive only after manual Stripe dashboard review; do not delete historical objects needed by old sessions or receipts.
Public checkout can be rolled back by reverting env vars to known-good price IDs and redeploying the previous backend commit.
rollbackNotesForLicenseGiftSubscriptionW: Before any job that mutates licenses, gifts, subscriptions, purchases, orders, webhook events, or analytics, take a database snapshot/export.
If webhook fulfillment creates incorrect licenses or orders, stop webhook replay, disable checkout if necessary, restore from snapshot or apply a targeted compensating update from the audit trail.
If gift or direct package flows create incorrect records, revoke only the affected pending packages/codes and preserve redeemed audit history.
If subscription sync updates the wrong expiry, restore affected license rows from backup or Stripe invoice/subscription period evidence.
duplicatePreventionNotes: Stripe setup finds existing products by fima_plan metadata or matching product name before creating new products.
Stripe setup finds existing active prices by product, currency, amount, one-time vs recurring type, and monthly interval before creating new prices.
Webhook fulfillment should continue to rely on unique Stripe session/event IDs and existing order/license records to avoid double fulfillment.
Gift redemption and direct package claim paths should continue to rely on existing redemption/package status constraints.
idempotencyNotes: The standalone setup script is intended to be safe to rerun because it reuses existing matching products and prices.
Admin runtime setup should be rerun only after verifying Render env state and Stripe mode; it should write the same env values when products/prices already exist.
Checkout creation is not idempotent by itself and should not be used as a migration or verification loop without explicit test scope.
Webhook replay must be treated as idempotency-sensitive and should be limited to known event IDs after backup.
